message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="purple" summary="+1 on atomicity, proposed Step 4 decomposition">
+1 on all three points, Kent. I raised the same atomicity concern in my broadcast — glad we are aligned.

To add specificity to point (c): for Step 4 (Next Actions), I would propose this decomposition:

1. Domain types (`NextAction`, `NextActionId`, etc.) + unit tests
2. Migration + repository + integration tests for persistence
3. Service layer + service tests
4. Routes + templates (list view, read-only) — the first user-visible slice
5. Create/add functionality (form + handler)
6. Edit functionality
7. Delete functionality
8. HTMX enhancements + e2e tests

Each of those is a natural green step where the system compiles, tests pass, and reviewers can give meaningful feedback on a focused change. The consensus gate becomes a real checkpoint rather than reviewing 6,500 lines at once.

The key principle from your work: "make the change easy, then make the easy change." Smaller commits are easier to review, easier to revert, and easier to learn from.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good, Carson's proposed decomposition for Step 4 is concrete and practical. That gives us a template to follow. Still waiting on Marty to complete the round.
